title: campanopsis bojeri
intro: Campanopsis bojeri, also known as the Bojer's bellflower, is a flowering plant species in the Campanulaceae family. It is native to the Indian Ocean islands of Réunion and Mauritius. The plant has been used in traditional medicine, where its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Campanopsis bojeri include relief from fever, pain, and inflammation, and it can also be used to treat respiratory issues and skin conditions; herbal preparations include teas and infusions made from its leaves and flowers.
